authoritative_sources.title: OSHA eTool: Construction - Trenching and Excavation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary U.S. construction safety regulator guidance translating 29 CFR 1926 excavation duties into practical competent-person inspection and stop-work expectations that directly govern site-readiness and safety escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Site safety prerequisite and inspection gating
used_for: boundary between controls review and safety authority

authoritative_sources.title: 2022 Construction General Permit (CGP)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal permit baseline for construction stormwater inspections, inspection report retention, site availability of records, and noncompliance escalation.

authoritative_sources.title: P120 Project Cost and Schedule Management Requirements for the Public Buildings Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal construction schedule-governance directive establishing baseline, re-baseline, milestone, and contractor CPM schedule handling that project controls specialists rely on.

authoritative_sources.title: Use our project schedule management for small projects
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official public workflow guidance for schedule creation, baseline timing, stakeholder cadence, and delay drivers relevant to smaller construction projects and field work.

authoritative_sources.title: Construction Manual, Section 5-1: Project Records and Reports
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official owner-side construction administration manual that specifies daily reports, change-order records, extra work bills, and project-record controls used in field execution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Daily records, change-order, and extra-work evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: Guidance - Project Management Plans - Major Projects
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FHWA major-project guidance defining closeout planning, record-management expectations, settled financial obligations, and transition from construction to operations.

authoritative_sources.title: Project Funds Management Guide for State Grants Q&A
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FHWA closeout reference defining when closeout begins and what final construction records, retainage, and reconciliation evidence are required.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Final construction record and closeout readiness

workflow_stages.name: Site intake and prerequisite capture
workflow_stages.description: Confirm project, site, work-order, trade, permit, and jurisdiction context before any control recommendation is made.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Safety, permit, and inspection evidence assembly
workflow_stages.description: Collect the source-of-truth permit records, inspection reports, safety observations, and site logs needed to determine whether work can proceed or remain on hold.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Schedule, change-order, and field-record control
workflow_stages.description: Validate baseline schedule, update cadence, delay or recovery context, daily reports, and change-order evidence before recommending status movement.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Hold, release, and incident boundary enforcement
workflow_stages.description: Apply control gates to release requests, recurring defects, and safety or quality incidents without overriding approved authority.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closeout and owner escalation
workflow_stages.description: Require closeout artifacts, final records, and owner assignments before recommending completion, beneficial occupancy, or financial closure posture.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

artifacts: Work orders and project control records
Permit status records, SWPPP or permit attachments, and inspection reports
Daily reports, site logs, safety observations, and nonconformance records
Baseline schedules, milestone updates, recovery plans, and delay notes
Change orders, extra work bills, subcontractor documentation, and punch lists
Closeout packages, O&M manuals, as-builts, and final acceptance records
Corrective-action, incident, and hold or release logs
systems_of_record: Schedule management tools and baseline files
Tenant authority matrices and policy libraries

allowed: Assess whether available site, permit, inspection, schedule, and closeout evidence supports hold, release, investigation, or escalation posture.
Produce evidence-backed quality or safety summaries, control maps, and owner-routed exception packages.
Request missing prerequisites and source-of-truth records before continuing.
escalate: Permit status, inspection requirements, or site conditions materially change the answer.
A recurring defect, incident, or closeout blocker requires formal QA, safety, superintendent, or trade ownership.
Tenant policy and field practice conflict, or jurisdictional rules are missing or contradictory.
refuse: Permit approval, code interpretation, or regulator-facing commitments
Licensed trade signoff, engineer-of-record judgment, or design approval
Release, closeout, or incident closure without sufficient evidence

domain_failure_modes: Treating a generic project status update as sufficient while ignoring actual permit, inspection, or site-readiness prerequisites.
Recommending release when a documented hold, failed inspection, or contradictory field evidence remains unresolved.
Collapsing construction controls into code interpretation, licensed trade judgment, or financial approval authority.
Closing work without final construction records, punch-list resolution, or closeout package completeness.
Ignoring change-order, extra-work, or schedule-baseline evidence that materially affects the control outcome.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that implies licensed trade or code-interpretation authority
Safety or quality evidence is incomplete or contradictory
Release is requested while a hold or unresolved issue exists
Incident handling requires licensed, safety, or formal QA authority
A required control is missing evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Jurisdictional permit rules, closeout definitions, and retained documentation can vary materially by locality, owner, and contract family.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant-supplied jurisdiction, contract type, and authority matrix at runtime before recommending final release or closeout posture.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Organizations differ on whether a failed inspection, punch item, or missing as-built is a hard hold, conditional release, or internal escalation.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Retrieve tenant policy and approval thresholds before any final recommendation and escalate if the tenant model conflicts with the public baseline.

notes: The research set centers the lane on construction evidence control rather than generic status reporting.
Where permit or code rules become jurisdiction-specific, the lane records the ambiguity and routes to qualified owners instead of guessing.
